## Skill name

<!-- What would the slash command be? (e.g., /diary-study-synthesis) -->

## Research phase

<!-- Which phase does this skill serve?
PLAN / COLLECT / SYNTHESIZE / EVALUATE / REPORT / QUERY / META -->

## Pipeline position

**Receives from:**
<!-- What skill outputs feed this skill? What files or data structures does it expect?
Check PIPELINE.md for the existing handoff chain. -->

**Produces for:**
<!-- What downstream skills can consume this skill's output?
What files or data structures does it produce? -->

## What gap this fills

<!-- What can researchers not currently do with the 16 core skills that this skill would enable?
Be specific — "better synthesis" is not a gap. "Synthesizing longitudinal diary entries across 30 days into emotional arcs" is. -->

## Your positionality

<!-- What is your relationship to this research method?
Are you a practitioner who has run this type of study? A researcher adapting a method from a different field?
Someone working in a context where the existing skills don't fit?

This isn't a credential check. It helps reviewers understand what assumptions are built in and what blind spots to watch for. -->

## How it applies the CORE.md principles

For each principle, describe how this skill applies it — not as a preamble but as a specific constraint in the workflow:

**Audit Before Acting:**

**Context Before Compression:**

**Edge & Variance Scan:**

**Care Check:**

**Plural Interpretations:**

## Example input → output

The example should demonstrate the skill at its most complex or ambiguous case, not its simplest. -->

```
[paste here]
```

**Output:**
```
[paste here]
```
